at_a_glance: You have spent $14.79 across 22 sessions over 10 days building Promptly from scratch. The most expensive single session was session 020 at $1.35, covering 26 tasks including FEATURE-017/018, BUG-033, and FEATURE-020 — the densest feature day of the project. Today's session (session 022) at $1.22 was the second highest, driven by a large ExpandedView refactor that split a 1131-line component into four files plus a review subagent. All formal tasks are complete and the project is at 100%.

session_narratives.2026-04-18-001: The planning session — brainstorm, architect, and spec-review for the entire app. Expensive per task ($0.20 avg) because these skills read the entire brief and produce large structured specs.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-002: All 9 Phase 1 foundation tasks plus the phase review in one session. The phase-1-review alone cost $0.25 — reading every file produced so far. This set the baseline for all future sessions.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-003: Planning-only session for F-STATE. No code committed. vibe-add-feature + vibe-spec-review produced detailed FEATURE_TASKS specs, cost $0.47.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-004: Built the full state machine (all 5 FST tasks). Medium context at this stage kept per-task cost reasonable at ~$0.10.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-005: Planning session for F-FIRST-RUN. Cost slightly higher than F-STATE planning because CODEBASE.md was now populated and loaded on each turn.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-006: Finished just FRN-004 (the last first-run task). Low cost because only one task; the rest of the session was a vibe-cost run.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-007: Design implementation + 6 bug fixes + mode dropdown fix. The design task alone was $0.27 — a large L-size output generating CSS, animations, and token overhauls.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-008: DECISION-004 (recording pill removal) + BUG-004. Two complex refactors; $0.42 total, reasonable for the structural change involved.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-009: Vibrancy, BUG-007 (two sub-bugs), and FEATURE-001 splash screen in one session. The splash screen was a new BrowserWindow + full vanilla HTML — the $0.25 L task reflects that.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-010: Phase 2 review + all P1/P2/P3 backlog fixes. The phase-2-review at $0.31 was the most expensive single task to date — it read the entire Phase 2 output.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-011: Final review gate + console cleanup. Review at $0.285 reading all source files. After this the app was cleared for distribution.
session_narratives.2026-04-19-012: Phase 4 kickoff — React migration cleanup, F-HISTORY and F-LANGUAGE removal. Context was now large ($0.76 for 5 tasks).
session_narratives.2026-04-19-013: Short session — just startup reads and a vibe-cost run. $0.165 for essentially no output.
session_narratives.2026-04-20-014: FEATURE-011 pause/resume recording. vibe-add-feature planning dominated cost at $0.225; three build tasks at $0.10 each.
session_narratives.2026-04-20-015: DESIGN-001 vibrancy + ambient glow. Light session, one visual task. $0.35 mostly on the design work itself.
session_narratives.2026-04-20-016: The project's most technically dense session — iteration mode, 5 bug fixes, and app signing. $1.17 for 7 tasks; FEATURE-012 alone was $0.30 as the most complex feature so far.
session_narratives.2026-04-23-017: BUG-017 (nvm PATH resolution) + full FEATURE-014 text input + review gate. The bug was L-size deep codebase work; 12 tasks in one session kept per-task cost to $0.07.
session_narratives.2026-04-23-018: All 7 Polish mode tasks + feature-015 review + BUG-018/019 + bundle size. Efficient session — 13 tasks at $0.87 means $0.067/task average.
session_narratives.2026-04-24-019: FEATURE-016 uninstaller + FEATURE-013 path config panel. 16 tasks including new IPC handlers, native dialogs, and splash HTML changes. $0.87.
session_narratives.2026-04-24-020: The largest session by task count — 26 tasks spanning FEATURE-017/018, BUG-033 (App.jsx SRP split), FEATURE-020, and the final review gate. $1.35 total, $0.052/task average — the most efficient session of the project.
session_narratives.2026-04-26-021: Light maintenance session — Electron menu fix, history panel review fixes, and release.sh. Only 3 tasks; $0.255 total.
session_narratives.2026-04-27-022: Heavy ExpandedView day — visual polish (BUG-TOGGLE-003/004), full history parity (search, tabs, filters, stats), scale-up to 1100x860, a vibe-review subagent pass, and a 1131-line component split into four files. 12 tasks; the review subagent (CP-04) and large context overhead (CP-01) pushed cost to $1.22.

recommendations.title: Zero prompt cache hits across all 22 sessions
recommendations.body: Every session shows cache_read_tokens = 0. CLAUDE.md is modified frequently (new Active Feature blocks added each session) which prevents prompt caching. Split CLAUDE.md into a stable core (architecture rules, code style, tech stack) and a dynamic section (active features, tasks). Cache the stable part — it changes rarely but loads every turn. At 30KB of CODEBASE.md plus CLAUDE.md, caching could save 20–30% on input costs per session.

recommendations.title: Context overhead now 30KB — mature project pressure building
recommendations.body: CODEBASE.md is 30,584 bytes and loaded every session turn alongside a very large CLAUDE.md with 18+ Active Feature blocks (many now complete). Completed feature blocks in CLAUDE.md add tokens without value — they are already captured in DECISIONS.md. Consider trimming CLAUDE.md Active Feature sections for features shipped more than two sessions ago. This would reduce per-session input overhead by an estimated 10,000–15,000 tokens.

recommendations.title: Review subagents multiply context cost
recommendations.body: This session used vibe-review which spawned a subagent. Each subagent gets its own full context window — effectively doubling the session context cost for that segment. The review-expandedview task is estimated at $0.24, the most expensive single task this session. Reviews are valuable; just be aware that each review subagent is roughly equivalent to running a fresh mid-size session.
recommendations.saving: no change recommended — reviews are worth it

forecast_narrative: All formal tasks are complete — build progress is 100%. The only item remaining is a manual smoke test of the expanded view iteration flow. At current session rates, maintaining the app (future bug fixes or minor features) would cost roughly $0.25–$0.80 per session. If you start a significant new feature phase, expect $0.80–$1.35/session based on Phase 4 history. Implementing prompt caching (CP-05 fix) before any new feature work begins would reduce that by an estimated $0.20–0.30/session.
